programs.name: Modern Software & AI Engineering
programs.tagline: Software Engineering hasn't changed. What it takes to be great at it has. Stronger fundamentals, faster delivery, and AI fluency built into how you learn — not just an add on.
ideal_for: SDEs, QA Engineers & Frontend Developers (1-4 YoE) — build AI-aware engineering skills that move you beyond execution-only roles
Fullstack Developers, Backend & API Engineers (2-6 YoE)
Software Engineers, Senior Engineers & Tech Leads (3-8 YoE)
Solution Engineers & AI-First Product Builders (5-10+ YoE)

common_objections.objection: Why pay ₹3.5L when Andrew Ng has the same stuff for free on Coursera?
common_objections.grounded_handle: Coursera and Andrew Ng teach you the concepts. Scaler teaches you to ship. The difference is: (1) live mentors who are active engineers at Amazon, Google, Booking.com — not recorded professors; (2) evaluated projects on real business cases (Swiggy, Meesho, Myntra data) with AI integration built in; (3) cohort accountability and structured pacing — most Coursera learners never finish; (4) career support and placement infrastructure that free courses don't have. The curriculum moves quarterly as the market does. You can confirm specifics with us.

common_objections.objection: What salary jump can I realistically expect? If I go from 14 LPA at TCS to 16 at another service company, the math doesn't work.
common_objections.grounded_handle: The jump from services to product is real but not instant. The program targets product company roles, not lateral service-to-service moves. Specific salary data varies by person and we'll share placement reports — but the pitch is never 'slightly higher salary at a service company'. It's the switch from services trajectory to product trajectory, which compounds differently over 5–10 years. We can walk you through the placement report numbers on a follow-up call.

common_objections.objection: I want to build real LLM applications — RAG, agents, evals. Is your program on that, or more theoretical ML?
common_objections.grounded_handle: The Academy program has a Generative AI specialisation that covers RAG, multi-agent orchestration, and production AI systems — not just theory. There's a dedicated module 'AI & Agents: From Talking to AI to Building One' covering prompt engineering, RAG, and agentic systems. The Advanced AIML program goes even deeper — that's its core focus. The curriculum is updated quarterly. These are built-in, not add-ons.

common_objections.objection: Can you guarantee I'll get a job after this?
common_objections.grounded_handle: Scaler has strong placement outcomes and an active placement team, but we don't use the word 'guarantee' — no honest program does. What we do have: a dedicated placement team, alumni network of 1,00,000+, structured interview prep including AI mock interviews, and a track record of placements at product companies. The right question is: what does the placement report show for someone with a profile like yours? We can walk through that specifically.

common_objections.objection: ₹3.5L is more than what my family earns in a year. I don't know how people afford this.
common_objections.grounded_handle: Scaler has financing options including income share agreements and EMI plans that make the upfront cost lower. The program is designed specifically so the financial commitment doesn't have to come upfront. This is a common concern and the advisors have walked many students in similar situations through the options — worth a follow-up call to get the specifics for your situation.

common_objections.objection: I'm nervous about the entrance test. What if I can't clear it?
common_objections.grounded_handle: The entrance test is designed to assess learning potential, not current knowledge. It's not about how much you already know — it's about whether you can learn. There's prep material available, and the test has multiple attempts. If someone is serious about the program, the test is a solvable step, not a filter for only the already-expert. Specific test format and prep resources can be shared by the advisor.

common_objections.objection: Is your cohort going to be at my level? If I'm tutoring everyone, I'm not getting value.
common_objections.grounded_handle: Scaler has different tracks within the program — the Advanced track is designed for people who already have strong DSA foundations. Senior folks from Google, Microsoft, and similar companies do enrol. The program is structured so more experienced engineers are in cohorts with others at comparable levels. Specific cohort composition for the next batch is something the advisor can walk you through.

common_objections.objection: Are your instructors people who've actually shipped production AI systems, or is it academic?
common_objections.grounded_handle: The instructors are active practitioners — not academics. Examples: Anshuman Singh (Co-Founder, built Facebook Messenger), Umang Agrawal (SDE-II Amazon, 750+ sessions), Alok Singh (built Booking.com's Eligibility API serving 5M+ partners at 99.99% uptime), Saurabh Kango (ex-LinkedIn Analytics Lead, ex-Airbnb Data Scientist), Thanish Batcha (built end-to-end ML at Amazon using BERT and RoBERTa). These are people currently working at or recently from companies you'd want to join.

common_objections.objection: My family wants me to take a government job. Can Scaler compete with that security?
common_objections.grounded_handle: This is genuinely a values question, not just a salary question. Government jobs offer security and stability — Scaler can't replicate that guarantee. What Scaler offers is a different kind of trajectory: higher earning potential in the medium term, more dynamic work, and a path to roles in product companies. The honest framing: if security is the #1 priority, a government job might be right. If growth trajectory and work type matter equally, this is worth exploring. The advisor can help think through the comparison honestly.

platform_differentiators.claim: AI built into every module — not bolted on after
platform_differentiators.detail: Every module runs on structured AI workflows (Prompt → Review → Own for engineering; Generate → Validate → Improve for data science). 24×7 AI Companion that hints, critiques, and pair-programs. Updated quarterly.

platform_differentiators.claim: Instructors and mentors are active industry practitioners, not academics
platform_differentiators.detail: Mentors are currently employed, currently hiring. They review your work and bring judgment from inside the teams you want to join.

platform_differentiators.claim: Lifelong learning access — curriculum updates forever at no extra cost
platform_differentiators.detail: When the industry shifts, your curriculum shifts with it. You're not buying a 2026 snapshot. You're buying a living system.

platform_differentiators.claim: Every project goes from raw data to a deployed, AI-integrated system
platform_differentiators.detail: Projects are interview-ready and AI-integrated — something you can open, walk through, and defend including the AI features you built into it.
Structured check-ins, career support, and exclusive access to 1,00,000+ Scaler alumni. The community outlasts the curriculum.

platform_differentiators.claim: AI Mock Interviews — practice with avatars trained on real interview formats
platform_differentiators.detail: Case-based, reasoning-first formats across DSA, LLD, system design, and data science. Walk into the room ready.

market_context.claim: 163% growth in AI-related job postings between 2024 and 2025 — even as overall tech hiring fell
'AI Engineer' ranked as the fastest-growing job title in the US on LinkedIn's 2026 Jobs Report
